title: Endo H/ PNGase F Assay for JESS Automated Western Blot
author: Charis Ma

steps_raw.instruction: In an eppendorf tube, add 40 µg protein, 2 µL 10X Glycoprotein Denaturing Buffer, and water for a total reaction volume of 20 µL . Mix well.
Denature glycoprotein by heating reaction at 100 °C for 00:10:00 .
Split reaction volume into 2 eppendorf tubes with 10 uL in each tube. One tube will be used for the EndoH reaction, while the other will be used for the PNGaseF reaction.
Add the following to one reaction tube from step 3: 2 µL 10X GlycoBuffer 3, 2 µL Endo H, 6 µL water. Total volume will be 20 µL .
Add the following to the second reaction tube from step 3: 2 µL 10X GlycoBuffer 2, 2 µL NP-40, 6 µL water, 1 µL PNGaseF. Total volume will be 21 µL .
Add 480 µL and 479 µL of 0.1X Sample Buffer (Bio-Techne) to the Endo H and PNGaseF reaction tubes respectively to make the final volume 500 µL .
Add all 500 µL of each reaction to a 10K Amicon Ultra Centrifugal Filter
Spin at 14000 x g, °C, for 00:20:00 . You will recover a final volume of 20 µL , and the protein concentration recovered will be ~ 1 mg/mL .
Add 4 parts of the washed Endo H/ PNGaseF sample from step 12 with 1 part of 5X Fluorescent Master Mix (Bio-Techne).
Load into JESS microplate (Bio-techne) to be detected by the Automated Western Blot System.
